reforms.description: The Colombian government aims to enhance investment in innovation by providing more grants and loans for R&D to enterprises, modifying project evaluation criteria for fiscal benefits, and introducing tax incentives that allow companies to deduct 100% of related expenditure from their tax base and an additional 25% from the tax due.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These measures are designed to stimulate private sector investment in R&D and innovation, which is expected to enhance productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax incentives and modified evaluation criteria have been put into practice, as indicated by the implementation year.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms significantly enhance the funding landscape for R&D but do not represent a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: As a result of the 2016 tax reform, companies that invest in activities that qualify as research, technological development or innovation are able to deduct 100% of related expenditure from their tax base.

reforms.description: Colciencias has initiated the Innovation Management Support Programme, providing grants directly to private firms to strengthen their innovation capacity through various innovation-related practices.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This program is expected to enhance the innovation capabilities of firms, leading to increased productivity and competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The program has been actively providing grants since its inception.
reforms.importance_rationale: The program represents a significant effort to support innovation in the private sector but is not a major systemic change.
reforms.source_quote: In 2011 Colciencias started to provide grants directly to private firms to strengthen their innovation capacity through knowledge management, strategic innovation management, rethinking business models and other innovation-related practices.

reforms.description: The Colombia Científica programme aims to improve the quality of higher education institutions by supporting scientific ecosystems that link academia and industry.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the quality of higher education and fostering collaboration between academia and industry, the program is expected to build a more skilled workforce and promote innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The program has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The program represents a significant investment in human capital for innovation but does not constitute a major overhaul of the education system.
reforms.source_quote: The Colombia Científica programme has recently been designed to improve the quality of the higher education institutions through the support of scientific ecosystems.

reforms.description: Colombia plans to double its expenditure on science, technology, and innovation from 0.5% of GDP in 2015 to 1% of GDP by 2018, aiming to enhance research and innovation activities in the private sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Doubling the expenditure on R&D is expected to significantly boost research activities and innovation in the private sector, leading to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan to double expenditure has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in the funding of R&D in Colombia.
reforms.source_quote: The government plans to double expenditure on science, technology and innovation from 0.5% of GDP in 2015 to 1% of GDP by 2018.

reforms.description: The establishment of iNNpulsa in 2012 aims to promote innovative businesses by allocating co-financing grants to start-ups and strengthening the environment for credit allocation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By supporting start-ups and improving access to credit, the initiative is expected to foster innovation and entrepreneurship, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been actively promoting innovative businesses since its establishment.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to support start-ups but does not constitute a major systemic change.
reforms.source_quote: Another important tool for promoting innovation and new business creation has been the establishment of iNNpulsa in 2012.

reforms.description: The Colombian government has established the National Policy for Productive Development to enhance firms' productivity through strategies focused on knowledge and technology transfer, innovation, and entrepreneurship, addressing impediments to increased productivity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This policy aims to improve R&D investment and foster innovation links between the business sector and research institutions, which is expected to enhance productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The policy has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a national policy represents a significant structural change in how productivity and innovation are approached in Colombia.
reforms.source_quote: In 2016, the government established the National Policy for Productive Development to address the impediments to increased productivity.

reforms.description: The reform of the General System of Royalties (SGR) has improved the distribution of royalty revenues from the oil and mining sector, allowing for increased investment in research and development projects, education, and infrastructure in poorer regions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing investment in R&D and education in poorer regions, this reform is expected to promote regional development and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been enacted and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the allocation of resources for R&D and education.
reforms.source_quote: The reform of the General System of Royalties (SGR) in 2011-12 has reduced these inequalities by sharing royalty revenues from the oil and mining sector more evenly across departments.

reforms.description: The Colombian government has implemented a National Financial Inclusion Strategy aimed at promoting access to financial services, particularly for informal workers, by providing microcredit and enhancing the use of electronic payments.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving access to financial services, this strategy is expected to support the growth of start-ups and innovative businesses.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy has been actively promoting financial inclusion since its implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This strategy represents a significant structural change in the approach to financial inclusion and support for start-ups.
reforms.source_quote: The National Financial Inclusion Strategy’s goal is to promote access and use of financial services, in particular among informal workers.
